spec_match.rationale: Uses the current WebCodecs contract: numeric rotation plus boolean flip. The support probe constructs a buffer-backed VideoFrame, verifies rotation=90, flip=true, and the displayWidth/displayHeight swap.

interactive.rationale: Rotation and flip controls, a render button, and the Android front camera preset all update live canvases and metadata readouts. DevTools MCP exercised the 90-degree + horizontal mirror preset.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The page promises raw-versus-oriented rendering from VideoFrame metadata, and the verified path reports constructor values, readback values, coded/display dimensions, and the native drawImage(VideoFrame) render path.

edge_cases.rationale: Covers default, rotated, and front-camera mirrored cases; surfaces fallback state when metadata is unsupported; and links to sibling pages for active-orientation lock, rotation rounding, and codec carriage.

references.rationale: Includes the ChromeStatus feature 5128855343595520 and WebCodecs spec references.

design_system.rationale: Uses design-system variables for UI chrome and keeps raw colors inside bitmap drawing only.

summary: Verified in Chrome 148 with chrome-devtools-mcp: Android front camera preset produced rotation=90, flip=true, coded 200x280, display 280x200, and a native VideoFrame canvas render with no console errors.
